perturbation.original_text: d)All sales made by the Agent of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products shall create a Book of Business owned by the Agent, which constitutes all funds due to Agent under this agreement flowing from each sale for a period not to exceed 36 months from the date of acceptance by Kallo of the Client EULA or until the EULA terminates under the provision that are outlined therein of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products.
perturbation.changed_text: d) All sales made by the Agent of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products shall create a Book of Business, which the Agent manages in accordance with prevailing market conditions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause specifying that the Book of Business and its associated funds are 'owned by the agent', and also removing the 36 month termination limit, the contract now creates an inconsistency. The original implied the agent had clear financial rights to a revenue stream, managed in a defined time, but the change now suggests the agent merely manages the Book of Business and does not necessarily own the profit from it. This is further contradicted in Section 7(c) i, ii,iii,iv,v, where it references the 'Agent's Book of Business' and how the funds should be handled, implying that the agent does own it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) On Termination: Notwithstanding any terms of this agreement to the contrary, upon termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever, Kallo, unless otherwise agreed, shall set aside the Agent's Book of Business to be held by Kallo. i.The revenue stream defined by the Book of Business at the time Kallo becomes the controlling party will remain constant except in as far as any elected reduction in use by its customers. Any increase in use will not be to the benefit of the Book of Business. ii.Kallo shall pay over all funds collected from the Book of Business and due to Agent, less a reasonable administration fee to be assessed and determined by Kallo, to Agent or its designees or assigns immediately upon receipt and act as a conduit for Agent funds, except in as far as Kallo or its assignees are required to support customers in the Book of Business in which case the cost of providing such support, will be reasonably assessed and appropriate deductions made before passing the balance to Agent. In the event Agent does not agree with the costs described in this section, the issue will be submitted to binding arbitration. iii.Kallo shall make an accounting of funds collected to Agent or their assigns monthly. Kallo shall permit Agent or its designated Agent(s) to inspect Kallo's books upon Agent's request at Agent's expense during normal business hours of Kallo. iv.Kallo's failure to perform its duties in the event it becomes the controlling party of the Agent's Book of Business is a non-material breach of this agreement. v.In any dispute or litigation over the terms of this agreement, the prevailing party shall have reasonable attorney fees in addition to any settlement or damages awarded.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit statement that the 'Book of Business' is owned by the agent, and the 36 month limit. This directly contradicts section c, which repeatedly references 'Agent's Book of Business' and how funds should be handled, implying ownership. The removal of ownership and time limitations creates a direct conflict with the assumption of ownership and time limitation in section (c). The explicit change breaks compatibility with the contradicted text, creating a legal issue in the document based on the provided information and definitions of Omissions.

perturbation.original_text: Certification is mandatory for Agent to become certified to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products licensed under this agreement and to benefit from commissions allocated to Agent as outlined in Schedule A of this agreement. Non-compliance of this Section (2), subparagraph (c) on the part of the Agent will automatically void this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Certification is encouraged for the Agent to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite of products. Compliance of this Section (2), subparagraph (c) on the part of the Agent will provide benefits allocated to Agent.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the wording from 'mandatory' to 'encouraged', the contract now introduces an in-text contradiction. It removes the imperative that the agent must become certified, but elsewhere the contract continues to operate under the assumption that the agent has achieved this certification. This contradiction casts doubt on whether the Agent can represent Kallo Mobile care suite of products, as the term 'Certified Sales Agent' is defined in the 'Definitions' section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Certified Sales Agent” means a Sales Agent who has completed the initial Sales and Technical Training program and any consecutive KALLO - MOBILECARE Page 3 training program provided and mandated by Kallo and who abides by the terms and conditions of the certification program as outlined herein.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates certification for the Agent to represent the Kallo Mobile care suite, with non-compliance voiding the agreement. The changed text encourages certification but removes the mandatory requirement. However, the contradicted text defines 'Certified Sales Agent' as someone who 'has completed the initial Sales and Technical Training program and any consecutive KALLO - MOBILECARE Page 3 training program provided and mandated by Kallo'. Because the changed text makes certification merely encouraged, it contradicts the definition of 'Certified Sales Agent', as it is now uncertain whether the Agent needs to complete the 'mandated' training to be considered a 'Certified Sales Agent'. This introduces a contradiction as to whether the Agent is a 'Certified Sales Agent' according to the original agreement.

perturbation.original_text: A.Mobile Clinic and Command Center maintenance is included in the Five year Standard Mandatory Maintenance as per the Program description given in Schedule E
perturbation.changed_text: A.Mobile Clinic and Command Center maintenance may be requested by the Agent at an additional cost to be determined at the time of the request
perturbation.explanation: This modification removes the previously included Five-year Standard Mandatory Maintenance from the agreement and now reuqires for the Agent to request maintenance at an additional cost. This creates a contradiction, because throughout the Schedule G, there are mentions of 'Standard Mandatory Maintenance Service for 5 years' for all the products. It is also not being mentioned that in order to get it, it needs to be requested.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SCHEDULE - G Kallo Mobile care suite of products (Price is subject to change based on the requirement and configuration of various systems in the Mobile Clinic, and is exclusive of taxes, levies custom duties, freight and insurance.) KALLO - MOBILECARE Description Kallo Price Agent Markup End user price Mobile Acute Care Clinic - Base price $2,650,000 $265,000 $2,915,000 NOTE: PRICING INCLUDES TELE PHARMACY SYSTEM COSTING $150,000/ - FOR EACH MOBILE CLINIC. Standard Mandatory Maintenance Service for 5 years $2,915,000 $1,457,500 $4,372,500 $1,722,500 $7,287,500 Description Kallo Price Agent Markup End user price Mobile Chemotherapy Clinic - Base price $1,850,000 $185,000 $2,035,000 Standard Mandatory Maintenance Service for 5 years $2,035,000 $1,017,500 $3,052,500 $1,202,500 $5,087,500 Description Kallo Price Agent Markup End user price Mobile Dialysis Clinic - Base price $1,600,000 $160,000 $1,760,000 Standard Mandatory Maintenance Service for 5 years $1,760,000 $880,000 $2,640,000 $1,040,000 $4,400,000 Description Kallo Price Agent Markup End user price Mobile HIV & Malaria Clinic - Base price $1,800,000 $180,000 $1,980,000 Standard Mandatory Maintenance Service for 5 years $1,980,000 $990,000 $2,970,000 $1,170,000 $4,950,000 Description Kallo Price Agent Markup End user price Command Center for Mobile Clinics $550,000 $55,000 $605,000 Standard Mandatory Maintenance Service for 5 years $605,000 $302,500 $907,500 $357,500 $1,512,500
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original agreement, which included a 'Five year Standard Mandatory Maintenance' for Mobile Clinics and Command Centers, to state that the Agent now has to request for maintenance at an additional cost. This creates a direct conflict with Schedule G, which lists the 'Standard Mandatory Maintenance Service for 5 years' as part of the Kallo Mobile care suite of products pricing. The change creates an omission by removing the previously included maintenance and requires the Agent to request it at an additional cost, contradicting the fixed pricing that is previously stated for all the mobile clinics, and command center in Schedule G.
